name: com.plm.action.ActionController
comment: Generic action controller — single entry point for all action dispatch.  URL pattern: {@code POST /api/psm/actions/{actionCode}/{id1}/{id2}/...} Path IDs are interpreted by the action's scope definition.

methods.name: extractPathIds
methods.comment: Extracts path segments after {@code /actions/{actionCode}/} (relative to the service context-path).

name: com.plm.action.ActionService
comment: Builds the enriched actions list for the server-driven UI payload.  Drives from the {@code action} catalog and {@code authorization_policy} as the sole bridge between actions and node types. For LIFECYCLE-scope actions, emits one entry per lifecycle transition wired for the node type.

methods.name: buildActionKey
methods.comment: Composite identifier emitted to the UI. Matches the path segment the client POSTs back in {@code POST /api/psm/nodes/{id}/actions/{id}}. For LIFECYCLE actions, includes the transitionId so the dispatcher knows which transition to fire.

methods.name: checkRequiredPermissions
methods.comment: Checks all required permissions for an action via {@link ActionConfig#requiredPermissions()}. Returns true only if ALL required permissions pass.

name: com.plm.action.ActionWrapper
comment:
Middleware in the action execution pipeline. Each wrapper receives the context, parameters, and a {@code next} callable representing the rest of the chain. The wrapper can:
  • Enrich the context (e.g., set txId)
  • Short-circuit (e.g., permission denied)
  • Wrap in try/catch (e.g., transaction commit/rollback)
Wrappers are algorithm beans and are attached to actions via {@code action_wrapper} configuration in the database.

methods.name: wrap
methods.comment: Wraps action execution.

name: com.plm.action.ActionWrapper$Chain
comment: Represents the rest of the wrapper chain + the final handler.

name: com.plm.action.PlmActionValidator
comment:
Startup validation for {@link PlmAction}-annotated service methods.

Validates that each {@code @PlmAction} code exists in ConfigCache and is a dispatchable action (has a handler). Permission-only codes should use {@code @PlmPermission} instead — those are validated by {@link PlmPermissionValidator}.

Also validates that {@code requiredPermissions} on actions reference known permission codes in ConfigCache.

name: com.plm.action.guard.ActionGuard
comment: PSM extension of the platform action guard. Adds {@code @AlgorithmType} for psm-admin algorithm catalog registration. All PSM guard implementations implement this interface.

methods.name: evaluate
methods.comment: Evaluates all effective guards for the given action context. Uses cached guard attachments -- no DB query.

methods.name: assertGuards
methods.comment: Asserts BLOCK guards pass at execution time. HIDE guards are UI-only and are NOT enforced here -- they control action visibility, not executability.

methods.name: evaluateSplit
methods.comment: Split evaluation for managed (managee) actions. HIDE guards resolved from hideActionId (the manager). BLOCK guards resolved from blockActionId (the managee itself).

methods.name: assertSplit
methods.comment: Split assertion for managed actions at execution time. Only BLOCK guards from blockActionId are enforced.

methods.name: resolveGuardFromConfig
methods.comment: Resolves a guard from ConfigCache data (replaces the old JOOQ-based resolveGuardFromRow). Looks up the algorithm code via instanceId → algorithmId → code, then resolves the Spring bean and collects instance parameters.

methods.name: resolveAlgorithmCode
methods.comment: Resolves algorithm code from an instance id: instanceId → AlgorithmInstanceConfig.algorithmId() → AlgorithmConfig.code()

name: com.plm.action.internal.ActionDispatcher
comment: Central action executor with middleware pipeline.  Resolves the action handler and wrapper chain from the algorithm registry, builds the execution pipeline, and runs it.  No direct knowledge of transactions, locks, or node services — all cross-cutting concerns are handled by wrappers (algorithm beans).

methods.name: dispatch
methods.comment: Dispatches an action using scope-driven ID resolution.

methods.name: resolveWrappers
methods.comment: Resolves ordered wrappers with their instance parameters from ConfigCache.

methods.name: resolveAlgorithmCode
methods.comment: Resolves the algorithm code for a given algorithm instance id via ConfigCache.

methods.name: buildChain
methods.comment: Builds a chain of wrappers ending with the handler.

name: com.plm.algorithm.AlgorithmBean
comment: Marks a Spring bean as an algorithm implementation.  The {@link #code()} must match the {@code algorithm.code} column in the database. At startup, {@link AlgorithmStartupValidator} cross-checks all annotated beans against DB rows — missing algorithms are auto-registered.  Also acts as {@link Component} so annotated classes are Spring beans.

methods.name: code
methods.comment: Must match {@code algorithm.code} in the database.

methods.name: name
methods.comment: Human-readable name. If empty, defaults to the simple class name.

methods.name: description
methods.comment: Description. If empty, defaults to empty string in DB.

name: com.plm.algorithm.AlgorithmParam
comment: Declares a parameter for an algorithm implementation.  Used on classes annotated with {@link AlgorithmBean} to declare the parameter schema. At startup, {@link AlgorithmStartupValidator} ensures matching rows exist in {@code algorithm_parameter}.

name: com.plm.algorithm.AlgorithmRegistry
comment: Discovers all {@link AlgorithmBean}-annotated beans at startup, wraps each in a timing proxy that records execution statistics, and indexes them by code.  Discovery runs in {@code @PostConstruct} (not constructor) to avoid circular dependency issues — action handlers depend on services that depend on AlgorithmRegistry.

methods.name: getInstance
methods.comment: Lazy accessor for services that cannot inject AlgorithmRegistry at construction time (breaks circular dependency). Use this instead of injecting AlgorithmRegistry via constructor.

methods.name: resolve
methods.comment: Resolves an algorithm bean by code, cast to the expected interface. The returned object is a timing proxy — all calls are measured.

methods.name: getModuleForCode
methods.comment: Spring Modulith module name for an algorithm code, or null if unknown.

methods.name: getDomainForCode
methods.comment: First-level sub-package (domain) within the module, or null.

methods.name: getModuleByCode
methods.comment: Immutable snapshot of code → module name mappings.

methods.name: computeDomain
methods.comment: Extract the first meaningful sub-package under the module base. com.plm.node.transaction.internal.handler → "transaction" com.plm.node.handler                       → "handler" com.plm.permission.internal                → "internal" skipped, returns null

name: com.plm.algorithm.AlgorithmRegistry$StatsInvocationHandler
comment: Dynamic proxy handler that measures execution time for every method call and records it in {@link AlgorithmStats}.

name: com.plm.algorithm.AlgorithmType
comment: Declares an algorithm type on an interface.  At startup, {@link AlgorithmStartupValidator} ensures a matching {@code algorithm_type} row exists in the database. Missing types are auto-registered.

methods.name: id
methods.comment: Stable ID for the algorithm_type row.

methods.name: name
methods.comment: Human-readable name.

methods.name: description
methods.comment: Description.

name: com.plm.algorithm.internal.AlgorithmStartupValidator
comment:
Validates algorithms at startup against ConfigCache (read-only).
  1. Scans interfaces for {@link AlgorithmType} → validates they exist in ConfigCache
  2. Scans beans for {@link AlgorithmBean} → validates matching algorithm exists in ConfigCache
  3. Scans beans for {@link AlgorithmParam} → validates matching parameters exist in ConfigCache
  4. Reverse check: ConfigCache algorithms without matching Spring beans

No longer writes to DB — admin tables are managed by psm-admin.

methods.name: syncAlgorithmTypes
methods.comment: Step 1: Discover @AlgorithmType annotations on interfaces and validate they exist in ConfigCache.

methods.name: syncAlgorithms
methods.comment: Step 2: Discover @AlgorithmBean annotations and validate matching algorithm exists in ConfigCache.

methods.name: syncParameters
methods.comment: Step 3: Validate @AlgorithmParam annotations match parameters in ConfigCache.

methods.name: reverseCheck
methods.comment: Step 4: Reverse check — ConfigCache algorithms without matching beans.

name: com.plm.algorithm.internal.AlgorithmStats
comment: Collects per-algorithm execution statistics in memory. Thread-safe — uses atomic operations for concurrent updates.  Periodically drained and shipped over NATS to psa by {@link AlgorithmStatsService}.

methods.name: drainAndReset
methods.comment: Resets counters and returns the snapshot before reset (for flush-and-clear).

name: com.plm.algorithm.internal.AlgorithmStatsService
comment: Drains in-memory algorithm execution stats every 60 seconds and publishes deltas via NATS to the central aggregator (psa). Publish is fire-and-forget — losing a window is acceptable.

name: com.plm.dashboard.DashboardController
comment: Tableau de bord utilisateur — deux endpoints distincts pour chargement parallèle.  GET /api/psm/dashboard/transaction  → résumé tx OPEN courante GET /api/psm/dashboard/workitems    → noeuds sur lesquels l'utilisateur peut agir

methods.name: getOpenTransaction
methods.comment: Résumé de la transaction OPEN courante. Retourne 204 si aucune transaction ouverte.

methods.name: getWorkItems
methods.comment: Derniers noeuds modifiés sur lesquels l'utilisateur a au moins une action disponible, triés par nombre d'actions décroissant.

name: com.plm.dashboard.internal.DashboardService
comment: Données agrégées pour le tableau de bord utilisateur.  Deux vues distinctes, chargeables indépendamment : - openTransaction : résumé de la transaction OPEN courante (noeuds modifiés) - workItems       : derniers noeuds modifiés (COMMITTED) sur lesquels l'utilisateur a au moins une action disponible, triés par nombre d'actions décroissant

name: com.plm.node.NodeActionContextProvider
comment: Resolves node context (nodeTypeId, currentStateId, lock state) for PlmActionAspect. Runs DB queries against node/node_version/plm_transaction/node_version_link tables.

name: com.plm.node.NodeController
comment: API REST pour les noeuds PLM.  Toutes les opérations d'écriture passent par le ActionController central :  POST /api/psm/actions/{actionCode}/{id1}/{id2}/... Header: X-PLM-Tx (requis si l'action a requires_tx = true) Body:   { "parameters": { ... } }  Ce contrôleur ne gère que les lectures.

methods.name: getDescription
methods.comment: Retourne le payload UI complet (attributs, actions disponibles, état, lock…). txId optionnel : si fourni, montre la version OPEN de la transaction (si owner).

name: com.plm.node.NodeService
comment: CRUD sur les noeuds PLM.  Point d'entrée pour toute modification : {@link #checkout}. Le checkout fait trois choses dans l'ordre : 1. Acquiert le lock sur le noeud (LockService.tryLock — atomique, sans tx) 2. Trouve ou crée une transaction PLM 3. Crée la version OPEN dans cette transaction (idempotent)  Opérations sans txId (lecture seule) : buildObjectDescription  createNode prend uniquement le logical_id (et un externalId optionnel) : la version initiale est créée OPEN, vide, dans la transaction courante de l'utilisateur. Les attributs sont alimentés ensuite via {@link #modifyNode} et la complétude (required/regex/enum) est vérifiée au commit.

methods.name: listNodes
methods.comment: Legacy overload for backward compatibility — returns page 0, size 50.

methods.name: fetchLinksAtVersion
methods.comment: Retourne tous les liens committed actifs pour un noeud source, dont la version source a un version_number <= maxVersionNum. La source du noeud est dérivée via source_node_version_id → node_version.node_id.

methods.name: checkout
methods.comment: Checkout d'un noeud : acquiert le lock, trouve ou crée une transaction, puis crée la version OPEN dans cette transaction.  Idempotent : si le noeud est déjà checké-out dans cette transaction, retourne l'id de la version OPEN existante sans en créer une nouvelle.

methods.name: modifyNode
methods.comment: Modifie le contenu d'un noeud dans le contexte d'une transaction explicite. Le noeud doit être préalablement checké-out (lock acquis).

methods.name: modifyNode
methods.comment: Overload without strategy — defaults to ITERATE.

methods.name: createLink
methods.signature: createLink(java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.lang.String)
methods.comment: Crée un lien entre deux noeuds dans une transaction. Delegates to {@link LinkService#createLink}.

methods.name: buildObjectDescription
methods.comment: Backward-compatible overload — no historical version pinning.

methods.name: buildObjectDescription
methods.comment: Builds the full UI payload for a node.

methods.name: getCurrentStateId
methods.comment: Returns the current lifecycle_state_id for a node, considering an open tx if provided.

methods.name: getChildLinks
methods.comment: Retourne les liens sortants du noeud (BOM / enfants).

methods.name: getParentLinks
methods.comment: Retourne les liens entrants vers ce noeud (Where Used / parents).

methods.name: deleteLink
methods.comment: Deletes a link by ID. Delegates to {@link LinkService#deleteLink}.

methods.name: updateLink
methods.signature: updateLink(java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.lang.String)
methods.comment: Updates target and/or link_logical_id of an existing link. Delegates to {@link LinkService#updateLink}.

methods.name: resolveAsNameValue
methods.comment: Resolves the as_name attribute value for a node version from ConfigCache + DB.

methods.name: findOpenVersionInTx
methods.comment: Retourne l'id de la version OPEN de ce noeud dans cette transaction, ou null.

methods.name: isTypeOrDescendant
methods.comment: Returns true if typeId equals expectedTypeId or has expectedTypeId as an ancestor (walking parent_node_type_id up the hierarchy).

methods.name: assertNoCycle
methods.comment: Delegates cycle detection to {@link GraphValidationService}.

methods.name: resolveCheckoutStrategy
methods.comment: Reads the version_policy of the node's NodeType and maps it to a VersionStrategy for use during checkout.  NONE    → VersionStrategy.NONE    (same revision.iteration, traceability only) ITERATE → VersionStrategy.ITERATE (iteration + 1, A.1 → A.2) RELEASE → VersionStrategy.REVISE  (new revision, A.x → B.1)

methods.name: findState
methods.comment: Finds a lifecycle state by its ID, searching across all lifecycles in the config cache.

methods.name: findStateName
methods.comment: Returns the display name of a lifecycle state, or null if not found.

methods.name: assertNotFrozen
methods.comment: Throws FrozenStateException if the node's current lifecycle state has metadata key "frozen" = "true". A frozen state prohibits content modifications (checkout / modifyNode). Lifecycle transitions and signatures are still allowed.

name: com.plm.node.baseline.internal.BaselineService
comment: Gestion des baselines PLM.  Principe : - Une baseline est une photo cohérente de la grappe à un instant T - Prérequis : le noeud racine doit être dans un état Frozen (garantit l'absence de modifications concurrentes pendant le tag) - Résolution eager des liens VERSION_TO_MASTER au moment du tag → fiabilité garantie à long terme - Les liens VERSION_TO_VERSION sont auto-documentés (pas d'entrée baseline)  Stratégie anti race-condition : Le Frozen cascade (appliqué avant la baseline) lock toute la grappe. Une fois Frozen, aucune nouvelle version ne peut être créée sur les noeuds enfants. Le tag de baseline est donc toujours cohérent.

methods.name: createBaseline
methods.comment: Crée une baseline sur un noeud racine.

methods.name: getBaselineContent
methods.comment: Retourne le contenu complet d'une baseline : pour chaque lien VERSION_TO_MASTER, la version résolue au moment du tag.

methods.name: compareBaselines
methods.comment: Compare deux baselines : retourne les noeuds dont la version a changé entre les deux.

methods.name: listBaselines
methods.comment: Liste toutes les baselines avec leur métadonnée (backward compat — page 0, size 50).

methods.name: listBaselines
methods.comment: Liste les baselines avec pagination.

methods.name: resolveAndRecord
methods.comment: Parcourt récursivement la grappe depuis un noeud et enregistre une entrée baseline pour chaque lien VERSION_TO_MASTER trouvé.

methods.name: assertNodeIsFrozen
methods.comment: Vérifie que le noeud est dans un état Frozen avant de créer la baseline.

methods.name: assignDomain
methods.comment: Assigns a domain to a node version. Validates no attribute name collision with node_type attributes or other assigned domains.

methods.name: unassignDomain
methods.comment: Unassigns a domain from a node version. Removes domain attribute values.

methods.name: getAssignedDomainIds
methods.comment: Returns assigned domain IDs for a node version.

methods.name: getAssignedDomains
methods.comment: Returns assigned domains with details for a node version.

name: com.plm.node.handler.CreateNodeActionHandler
comment:
CREATE_NODE action handler.

NODE_TYPE scope: receives nodeTypeId from ActionContext. Only the identity fields {@code _logicalId} (required) and {@code _externalId} (optional) are read from params — domain attributes are populated later via {@code update_node} before checkin. Any other param is ignored here.

name: com.plm.node.handler.NodeScope
comment: NODE scope — expects a single nodeId path segment.

name: com.plm.node.handler.NodeTypeScope
comment: NODE_TYPE scope — expects a single nodeTypeId path segment. Used for actions that target a node type rather than an existing node (e.g. CREATE_NODE).

name: com.plm.node.lifecycle.internal.LifecycleScope
comment: LIFECYCLE scope — expects nodeId + transitionId path segments.

name: com.plm.node.lifecycle.internal.LifecycleService
comment: Application des transitions de lifecycle.  txId obligatoire pour toute transition (comme toute opération d'authoring). La transition est une modification de type LIFECYCLE — elle ne change pas revision.iteration mais crée une nouvelle version technique pour la traçabilité.

methods.name: invalidateCache
methods.comment: Invalidate lifecycle cache — call after metamodel changes to states/transitions.

methods.name: getTransitionTargetStateColor
methods.comment: Returns the color of the target state for a given transition, or null.

methods.name: getCachedTransition
methods.comment: Returns cached transition by ID, or null.

methods.name: getCachedState
methods.comment: Returns cached state by ID, or null.

methods.name: applyTransition
methods.comment: Applique une transition de lifecycle. Guards (action + lifecycle) are evaluated by PlmActionAspect via ActionGuardService before this method proceeds — including TransitionLifecycleGuard bridge.

methods.name: applyTransitionInternal
methods.comment: Internal transition logic — no guard evaluation, no @PlmAction. Called by the public guarded version.

methods.name: executeCascade
methods.comment: Data-driven cascade: for each outgoing link that has a cascade rule whose parent_transition_id matches the transition just fired on the parent node, fire the configured child transition on eligible child nodes. No-op when no rules are defined for this transition.

methods.name: resolveTransitionToState
methods.comment: Resolves the to_state_id of a transition from ConfigCache.

name: com.plm.node.lifecycle.internal.guard.FromStateMatchGuard
comment: Action Guard: node must be in the transition's source state. Attached to TRANSITION by default.  Resolves the from_state_id from the lifecycle config and compares with the node's current state.

name: com.plm.node.lifecycle.internal.guard.LifecycleGuard
comment: Lifecycle guard — evaluates a precondition for a lifecycle transition. Pure PSM concept; registered with platform-api via {@link LifecycleGuardCatalogContribution}.

name: com.plm.node.lifecycle.internal.guard.LifecycleGuardCatalogContribution
comment: Contributes lifecycle guard algorithms to platform-api's registry at startup. Collected by {@code ActionCatalogRegistrationClient} via the generic SPI.

name: com.plm.node.lifecycle.internal.guard.LifecycleGuardContext
comment: Context passed to each lifecycle guard during evaluation.

methods.name: evaluate
methods.comment: Evaluates all effective guards for the given lifecycle transition. Uses cached guard attachments — no DB query.

methods.name: assertGuards
methods.comment: Asserts BLOCK guards pass at execution time. HIDE guards are UI-only and are NOT enforced here — they control transition visibility, not executability.

methods.name: resolveAlgorithmCode
methods.comment: Resolves the algorithm code for a given instance id by walking all algorithms.

name: com.plm.node.lifecycle.internal.guard.NotFrozenGuard
comment: Action Guard: node must not be in a lifecycle state that has a given metadata key set to "true".  Parameterized via algorithm instance parameter {@code meta_key} (defaults to "frozen"). Attached to CHECKOUT by default.

name: com.plm.node.lifecycle.internal.guard.TransitionLifecycleGuard
comment:
Bridge between the action guard system and lifecycle guards.

Attached to the TRANSITION action via {@code action_guard} table. When evaluated by {@link com.plm.action.guard.ActionGuardService}, delegates to {@link LifecycleGuardService} which evaluates transition-specific guards (AllSignaturesDone, AllRequiredFilled, etc.).

This bridges the two guard systems cleanly:

  • ActionService UI evaluation picks up lifecycle guard violations automatically
  • PlmActionAspect enforces lifecycle guards at execution time
  • No cross-module dependency — both live in the node module

name: com.plm.node.lifecycle.internal.stateaction.StateAction
comment: Algorithm interface for lifecycle state actions.  State actions execute side-effects when entering or exiting a lifecycle state. Unlike guards (which evaluate preconditions and return violations), state actions perform mutations and signal failure by throwing.

methods.name: code
methods.comment: Algorithm code, must match {@code algorithm.code} in DB.

methods.name: execute
methods.comment: Executes the state action.  @throws RuntimeException to abort the transition (TRANSACTIONAL mode) or to be logged and swallowed (POST_COMMIT mode)

name: com.plm.node.lifecycle.internal.stateaction.StateActionContext
comment: Context passed to each {@link StateAction} during execution.

name: com.plm.node.lifecycle.internal.stateaction.impl.CollapseHistoryAction
comment: Collapses revision iteration history when entering a state marked with a specific metadata key (default: "released").  Parameterized via {@code meta_key} — the action checks that the target state has this metadata key set to "true" before proceeding. This makes the action reusable across different lifecycle configurations.  Deletes ALL committed versions of the CURRENT revision except the one entering the release state. Versions pinned by a baseline or VERSION_TO_VERSION link are preserved. The surviving version's iteration is set to 0 (displayed as just the revision letter, e.g. "A").  Revision bump (A -> B) happens later at Revise, not at Release.

name: com.plm.node.link.internal.GraphValidationService
comment: Graph-level validation for the PLM node structure.  Currently provides cycle detection for the node link graph. Extracted from NodeService to keep structural-graph concerns separate from node CRUD.

methods.name: assertNoCycle
methods.comment: BFS from targetNodeId through the existing SELF-link graph. Only SELF links participate in cycle detection — links to other sources (files, external systems) never create node-graph cycles.  If sourceNodeId is reachable from targetNodeId via the SELF link graph, adding source→target would create a cycle.  @throws NodeService.CircularReferenceException if a cycle would be created

name: com.plm.node.link.internal.LinkScope
comment: LINK scope — expects a single linkId path segment. Used for actions that target an existing link (delete_link, update_link).

name: com.plm.node.link.internal.LinkService
comment: Manages node links: creation, deletion, update, and read queries.  Links are triplets {@code (target_source_id, target_type, target_key)} owned by a source {@code node_version}. Resolver-specific concerns (key parsing, target lookup, type compatibility, cycle detection) live in the {@link SourceResolver} bound to each Source. Generic concerns (cardinality, link_logical_id rules, fingerprint) stay here.

methods.name: getChildLinks
methods.comment: Outgoing links from a node (BOM / children). SELF-source links are JOINed for full target detail; non-SELF rows are routed through their resolver.

methods.name: getParentLinks
methods.comment: Incoming links to a node (Where Used / parents). SELF parents come from the SQL fast-path; cross-source references are collected by querying each non-SELF resolver's {@code findReferencesTo}.

name: com.plm.node.link.internal.handler.CreateLinkActionHandler
comment:
Dispatch handler for the {@code create_link} action. Parameters:
  • {@code linkTypeId} — required, identifies the link_type
  • {@code targetSourceCode} — optional, defaults to the link_type's declared {@code targetSourceId} (usually {@code SELF})
  • {@code targetType} — optional, defaults to the link_type's {@code targetType}
  • {@code targetKey} — required, opaque key passed to the resolver (for SELF: {@code logical_id} or {@code logical_id@version})
  • {@code linkLogicalId} — required user-facing link identifier
V2V is no longer wired here — if the caller wants V2V they pass {@code @N} inside {@code targetKey} and the SELF resolver handles it.

name: com.plm.node.metamodel.MetaModelCachePort
comment:
Port for metamodel cache — abstracts the source of resolved node types, domain attributes, and state rules.

Implementation: {@code ConfigCacheAdapter} backed by {@code ConfigCache} from platform-lib, populated by psm-admin config snapshots.

methods.name: get
methods.comment: Get a resolved node type by ID, or null if not found.

methods.name: getAll
methods.comment: Get all resolved node types keyed by ID.

methods.name: getDomainAttributes
methods.comment: Get resolved attributes for a domain.

methods.name: getAllDomainInfos
methods.comment: All domain infos keyed by ID.

methods.name: getStateRuleInfo
methods.comment: Returns the most specific state rule for a (contextNodeTypeId, attrDefId, stateId) triple. Returns null if no rule exists.

methods.name: invalidate
methods.comment: Invalidates the cache; next access triggers a rebuild. No-op for push-based implementations.

name: com.plm.node.metamodel.MetaModelCachePort$DomainInfo
comment: Domain info record (simplified from JOOQ Record).

name: com.plm.node.metamodel.MetaModelCachePort$StateRuleInfo
comment: State rule info (simplified from JOOQ Record).

name: com.plm.node.metamodel.internal.ConfigCacheAdapter
comment:
MetaModelCachePort implementation backed by {@link ConfigCache} from platform-lib. Reads config snapshots pushed by psm-admin.

Converts platform-lib DTOs ({@link NodeTypeConfig}, {@link AttributeConfig}) to psm-api model types ({@link ResolvedNodeType}, {@link ResolvedAttribute}).

name: com.plm.node.metamodel.internal.ValidationService
comment:
Two-tier attribute validation.

Identifier policy — the attribute code ({@code attr.id}, e.g. {@code ad-ssi-zone}) is the canonical identifier across the entire chain (DB column {@code attribute_def_id}, request payload keys, violation {@code attrCode}). The attribute label (e.g. {@code "Install Zone"}) is for human display only and appears in {@code Violation.message} text. The camelCase {@code attr.name} is never used to identify or display attributes — it is a metamodel internal that does not surface in the protocol.

Hard tier — {@link #assertWritable}: schema membership and value-shape against {@code data_type}. Always blocks: prevents inconsistent data from reaching the DB. Called pre-write by {@code NodeService}.

Soft tier — {@link #collectViolations} / {@link #assertNoViolations}: required, naming regex, enum allowed values, lifecycle editability, logical-id pattern. Single engine; non-blocking by default (returned as feedback by update), thrown by lifecycle transitions and enforced at commit by {@code PlmTransactionService}'s built-in PreCommitValidator.

methods.name: displayLabel
methods.comment: Display string for messages: {@code label} > {@code code}. The Java {@code name} is never used.

methods.name: lookupAttributeLabelGlobally
methods.comment: Best-effort scan of all node types and domains to find an attribute by code and return its declared {@code label}. Used only on the unknown-attribute error path so messages can carry the human-readable label when the attr exists somewhere in the model but is not bound to the current scope.

methods.name: collectViolations
methods.signature: collectViolations(java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.util.Map, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.util.Set)
methods.comment: Collect violations of soft constraints. Never throws.

methods.name: assertNoViolations
methods.signature: assertNoViolations(java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.util.Map, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.util.Set)
methods.comment: Throws if {@link #collectViolations} returns any violation.

methods.name: resolveAttributes
methods.comment: Resolve own + inherited + domain attributes for a node type / specific version.

methods.name: resolveWritableScope
methods.comment: Writable scope for {@link #assertWritable}: own + inherited attrs of the node type, plus domain attrs from any domain attached to any version of the node (committed or OPEN). The OPEN version may carry a freshly-attached domain whose attributes the caller is about to write — those must be admitted even though the last committed version did not yet include the domain.

name: com.plm.node.metamodel.internal.ValidationService$Violation
comment: Structured validation failure.

name: com.plm.node.metamodel.internal.guard.AllRequiredFilledGuard
comment: Lifecycle Guard: all required attributes for the target state must have values.  Checks attribute values against the target state's attribute_state_rule. Returns one violation per missing required field.

name: com.plm.node.resource.NodeBrowseContribution
comment:
Publishes one browse descriptor per psm node type. Each descriptor: - lists items via {@code /nodes?type={id}}; - resolves item detail via {@code /nodes/{id}/detail} — a uniform {@link com.plm.platform.browse.dto.DetailDescriptor} that the generic frontend editor can render directly.

Emitted in {@link PanelSection#MAIN} at high priority so psm sits at the top of the navigation panel.

name: com.plm.node.resource.NodeBrowseVisibility
comment: Filters psm node-type browse descriptors down to those the calling user is allowed to {@code READ_NODE} on. Same context-push pattern as {@link NodeResourceVisibility} — service-to-service visibility calls do not carry a user on the request thread, so the resolver pushes one transiently around the policy check.

name: com.plm.node.resource.NodeCatalogContribution
comment:
Publishes one resource descriptor per psm node type so the federated frontend can offer "create Document / Part / Assembly" through the same picker as dst's "upload data file".

The create form only collects identity fields ({@code _logicalId} required, {@code _externalId} optional). Domain attributes are filled later via the standard checkout/{@code update_node} flow and validated at checkin. This keeps node creation a single decisive act and defers content shaping to the versioned editing pipeline.

name: com.plm.node.resource.NodeResourceVisibility
comment:
Filters psm node-type create descriptors down to those the calling user is allowed to {@code CREATE_NODE} on. Bound to platform-api's federated catalog via the platform-lib {@link ResourceVisibilityResolver} SPI.

Service-to-service visibility calls do not carry a user context on the request thread (they are authenticated via {@code X-Service-Secret}), so the resolver pushes a transient {@link PlmSecurityContext} reflecting the inbound {@link ResourceVisibilityContext} for the duration of the policy check, then clears it.

name: com.plm.node.signature.internal.CommentService
comment: Manages free-text comments on node versions.  Comments are outside the PLM transaction system — they attach directly to a node_version (open or committed) and capture its SHA-256 fingerprint at post time for tamper evidence (same mechanism as node_signature.signed_version_fingerprint).  Threading: top-level comments have parent_comment_id = NULL; replies reference the parent. UI enforces max 1 level of nesting.  Attribute context: optional attribute_name pins a comment to a specific attribute.

name: com.plm.node.signature.internal.SignatureService
comment: Gestion des signatures électroniques.  Signatures attach directly to the current version — no new version created, no lock acquired, no PLM transaction required (like comments).

name: com.plm.node.signature.internal.guard.AllSignaturesDoneGuard
comment: Lifecycle Guard: all required signatures must be collected on the current version.  Signatures are valid only for the current (frozen) version — not aggregated across iterations. After release (new revision), the slate resets.

name: com.plm.node.signature.internal.guard.HasSignatureRequirementGuard
comment: Action Guard: at least one outgoing transition from current state must have signature_requirement rows. Otherwise SIGN action is not relevant. Attached to SIGN by default.

name: com.plm.node.signature.internal.guard.NotAlreadySignedGuard
comment: Action Guard: user must not have already signed the current version. Signatures are per-version — not aggregated across iterations.

name: com.plm.node.signature.internal.guard.SignatureRejectionGuard
comment:
Lifecycle Guard: checks for rejected signatures on the current version. Two modes controlled by instance parameter {@code mode}:
  • {@code NO_REJECTED} — blocks if any rejected signature exists (for Release)
  • {@code HAS_REJECTED} — blocks if no rejected signature exists (for Unfreeze)

name: com.plm.node.source.DataResolver
comment:
Source resolver that targets binary blobs hosted in the dst service.
  • {@code type} — convention: {@code "filetype"} (single supported type for v1)
  • {@code key} — the dst-minted UUID returned by the upload endpoint
Resolution and validation walk through the spe gateway via {@link ServiceClient} to {@code GET /api/dst/data/{id}/metadata}. The link itself is always already-pinned (data objects are immutable once written) — the resolver returns {@code pinnedKey} equal to the input key.

name: com.plm.node.source.SelfNodeResolver
comment:
Built-in resolver for the SELF source: link targets are nodes inside this PLM instance. Key format:
  • {@code logical_id} — V2M, master link, target is the latest committed version
  • {@code logical_id@versionNumber} — V2V, target is pinned to that exact version
Validation reproduces the legacy node-type-pair check (parent-chain compatible) and runs cycle detection through the link graph.

methods.name: hasAncestorOfType
methods.comment: BFS upward through the committed SELF-link parent hierarchy of {@code logicalId}. Returns true if any ancestor node has a type that is {@code expectedType} or a descendant of it. This allows linking to a part that lives inside an assembly when the link type declares nt-assembly as the allowed target type.

name: com.plm.node.transaction.TransactionController
comment: API REST pour la gestion des transactions PLM.  Commit/rollback passent par le ActionController central : POST /api/psm/actions/COMMIT/{txId} POST /api/psm/actions/ROLLBACK/{txId}  Ce contrôleur gère les opérations de consultation et d'ouverture.

name: com.plm.node.transaction.internal.LockService
comment: Lock pessimiste sur les noeuds PLM.  Le lock est porté par la table {@code node} (colonnes locked_by / locked_at), indépendamment du modèle de transaction et de versioning.  Noeud libre  → node.locked_by IS NULL Noeud locké  → node.locked_by IS NOT NULL  {@link #tryLock} et {@link #unlock} sont les SEULS endroits qui écrivent ces colonnes.  {@link NodeService#checkout} est le point d'entrée de haut niveau qui coordonne : tryLock → find/create transaction → create OPEN version.

methods.name: tryLock
methods.comment:
Tente d'acquérir le lock sur {@code nodeId} pour {@code userId}.

Utilise {@code SELECT … FOR UPDATE} pour une acquisition atomique (la ligne node est verrouillée au niveau base de données pour toute la durée de la transaction Spring courante, évitant les races concurrentes entre deux checkouts simultanés sur le même noeud).

  • Si {@code node.locked_by} est NULL → acquiert le lock.
  • Si {@code node.locked_by == userId} → idempotent, retour silencieux.
  • Si {@code node.locked_by} est un autre utilisateur → {@link LockConflictException}.

methods.name: tryLockCascade
methods.comment: Lock cascade : acquiert le lock sur {@code rootNodeId} et tous ses descendants reliés par des liens VERSION_TO_MASTER. Fail-fast au premier conflit.

methods.name: unlock
methods.comment: Libère le lock sur {@code nodeId} : efface locked_by et locked_at. Appelé au commit / checkin pour chaque noeud commité.

methods.name: isLockedByTx
methods.comment: Retourne true si le noeud est locké ET possède une version OPEN dans {@code txId}. Utilisé par les tests pour vérifier qu'un checkout a bien eu lieu.

methods.name: resolveV2MDescendants
methods.comment:
Resolves all VERSION_TO_MASTER descendants of a node using a single recursive CTE query. Returns the flat list of all descendant nodeIds (excluding the root itself).

Only SELF-source links participate in lock cascade: cross-source links (files, external systems) cannot be locked through psm-api. V2M is detected by the absence of an {@code @version} suffix in {@code target_key}.

name: com.plm.node.transaction.internal.LockWrapper
comment: Lock acquisition wrapper for ISOLATED actions.  Acquires a lock on the target node before the action executes, and releases it after (regardless of success/failure). Only activates when the context has a nodeId.

name: com.plm.node.transaction.internal.PlmTransactionService
comment: Gestion du cycle de vie des transactions PLM.  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │  Modèle de transaction PLM                                  │ │                                                             │ │  OPEN ──────► COMMITTED   (commit avec commentaire)         │ │    │                                                         │ │    └────────► ROLLEDBACK  (annulation explicite)            │ │                                                             │ │  Visibilité des node_version liées à la transaction :       │ │   OPEN        → owner + admins uniquement                   │ │   COMMITTED   → tout le monde                               │ │   ROLLEDBACK  → admins uniquement (traçabilité)             │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  Règles : - Un utilisateur ne peut avoir qu'une seule transaction OPEN à la fois - La transaction est créée explicitement ou automatiquement au premier checkin si aucune n'est ouverte - Le commit exige un commentaire non vide - Le commit libère tous les locks de la transaction - Le rollback marque les versions ROLLEDBACK et libère les locks - Les versions ROLLEDBACK restent en base pour l'audit (visibles admins)

methods.name: registerBuiltInHooks
methods.comment: Registers built-in validators wrapping the existing services.

methods.name: openTransaction
methods.comment: Ouvre une transaction explicitement. Échoue si l'utilisateur a déjà une transaction OPEN.

methods.name: commitTransaction
methods.comment: Commit partiel ou total.

methods.name: releaseNodes
methods.comment: Libère des noeuds spécifiques d'une transaction OPEN (partial rollback).  Pour chaque noeud : 1. Supprime les attributs de ses versions dans la tx 2. Supprime les versions elles-mêmes 3. Libère le lock  Si la transaction devient vide après l'opération, elle est supprimée.

methods.name: rollbackTransaction
methods.comment: Annule la transaction et supprime physiquement toutes les versions créées dedans.  Sémantique : "comme si rien ne s'était passé". 1. Vérifie ownership 2. Supprime les attributs des versions OPEN de cette tx 3. Supprime les versions OPEN de cette tx 4. Libère les locks 5. Supprime la transaction elle-même (pas de trace)  Après rollback, le noeud retrouve exactement son état avant le checkin.

methods.name: findOpenTransaction
methods.comment: Retourne la transaction OPEN de l'utilisateur courant, ou null.

methods.name: getTransaction
methods.comment: Retourne les détails d'une transaction. Applique les règles de visibilité : - OPEN : owner + admins uniquement - COMMITTED : tout le monde - ROLLEDBACK : admins uniquement

methods.name: listTransactions
methods.comment: Liste les transactions visibles par l'utilisateur courant. - COMMITTED → toutes - OPEN      → seulement les siennes (+ toutes si admin)

methods.name: getTransactionVersions
methods.comment: Retourne les node_version associées à une transaction, en appliquant les règles de visibilité.

methods.name: getTransactionNodes
methods.comment: Retourne un résumé des noeuds modifiés dans la transaction : une ligne par noeud (dernière version dans la tx), avec logical_id et node_type_name résolus depuis ConfigCache.  Utilisé par le panneau de navigation du frontend.

methods.name: isVersionVisible
methods.comment: Vérifie si une node_version est visible par l'utilisateur courant.  Règles : - tx_id = NULL           → version initiale, toujours visible - plm_transaction.status = COMMITTED  → visible par tous - plm_transaction.status = OPEN       → visible uniquement par owner de la tx + admins  Note : il n'y a pas de statut ROLLEDBACK — les versions annulées sont supprimées physiquement au rollback.

methods.name: getCurrentVisibleVersion
methods.comment: Retourne la version courante d'un noeud — la plus récente, COMMITTED ou OPEN. Toutes les versions sont désormais visibles par tous les utilisateurs.

methods.name: cleanStaleTransactions
methods.comment: Rollback automatique des transactions OPEN expirées (sans activité depuis plus de 24h → lock expiré mais tx pas fermée). Tourne toutes les heures.

methods.name: getOrComputeFingerPrint
methods.comment: Returns the stored fingerprint of a version, or computes it on-the-fly if not yet persisted (e.g. versions created before V8 migration).

name: com.plm.node.transaction.internal.TransactionWrapper
comment:
Transaction management wrapper. Manages PLM transaction lifecycle around action execution based on the configured {@code tx_mode} parameter:
  • {@code NONE} — pass-through, no transaction
  • {@code REQUIRED} — tx must exist (fail if missing)
  • {@code AUTO_OPEN} — find or create user's tx
  • {@code ISOLATED} — dedicated tx, auto-commit/rollback

name: com.plm.node.transaction.internal.TxScope
comment: TX scope — expects a single txId path segment.

name: com.plm.node.transaction.internal.guard.LockOwnerRequiredGuard
comment: Action Guard: current user must own the lock on the node. Attached to CHECKIN, UPDATE_NODE, CREATE_LINK, UPDATE_LINK, DELETE_LINK by default.

name: com.plm.node.transaction.internal.guard.NotLockedGuard
comment: Action Guard: node must not be locked by any user. Attached to CHECKOUT and TRANSITION by default.

name: com.plm.node.version.internal.FingerPrintService
comment: Computes a canonical SHA-256 fingerprint for a node_version.  The fingerprint captures the CONTENT of a version:  state={lifecycle_state_id} |attrs={attr_def_id}:{value};...   (sorted by attr_def_id) |links={link_type_id}:{target_source_id}:{target_type}:{target_key};... (sorted)  Previous version fingerprint is NOT included — it would make every version unique even with identical content, breaking the no-change guard. The previous_version_fingerprint column on node_version provides chain integrity verification separately.  Signatures are NOT included — they reference the fingerprint (signed_version_fingerprint), not the other way around.  Consistent ordering ensures two versions with identical content produce identical fingerprints regardless of insertion order.  Two versions are considered identical (no-op) when their fingerprints match. The fingerprint is stored on node_version.fingerprint at commit time.

methods.name: compute
methods.comment: Computes the fingerprint for a given node version.  Content-only: two versions with identical state, attributes, and links produce the same fingerprint, enabling no-change detection at checkin.

name: com.plm.node.version.internal.VersionService
comment: Création de versions de noeuds PLM.  Le txId est un paramètre de première classe : - Obligatoire pour toute opération d'authoring - La version créée est taguée OPEN jusqu'au commit de la transaction - Le lock doit déjà avoir été acquis via LockService.checkin(nodeId, userId, txId)  Règles de numérotation métier — pilotées par VersionStrategy : NONE    → même révision.itération (traçabilité pure) ITERATE → itération + 1  (A.1 → A.2)   — défaut pour ChangeType.CONTENT REVISE  → nouvelle révision, itération reset à 1  (A.x → B.1)  ChangeType (CONTENT|LIFECYCLE|SIGNATURE) reste pour l'audit trail uniquement.

methods.name: createVersion
methods.signature: createVersion(java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.lang.String, com.plm.shared.model.Enums$ChangeType, com.plm.shared.model.Enums$VersionStrategy, java.lang.String, java.util.Map, java.lang.String)
methods.comment: Crée une nouvelle version dans le contexte d'une transaction explicite.

methods.name: updateVersionAttributes
methods.comment: Met à jour les attributs d'une version OPEN existante (pas de nouvelle version créée). Utilisé quand le noeud est déjà checké-out dans la même transaction : on remplace les attributs en place plutôt que d'empiler une deuxième version OPEN.

methods.name: getCurrentVersionForTx
methods.comment: Version courante visible pour une transaction donnée : - Dernière version OPEN de cette tx sur ce noeud (si elle existe) - Sinon dernière version COMMITTED

methods.name: getCurrentVersion
methods.comment: Dernière version COMMITTED d'un noeud (version publique).

name: com.plm.node.version.internal.guard.FingerprintUnchangedGuard
comment: Action Guard: blocks action when the current OPEN version has no real changes compared to the previous version (fingerprint unchanged). Attached to CHECKIN by default with BLOCK effect.

name: com.plm.permission.PermissionRegistry
comment:
Read-only in-memory cache of the {@code permission} table.

Loaded at startup. Provides permission metadata lookups for:

  • Permission module: scope resolution, annotation validation, admin UI catalog
  • Action module: scope lookup via {@link PermissionCatalogPort}

Scope codes are plain strings (e.g. {@code "GLOBAL"}, {@code "NODE"}, {@code "LIFECYCLE"}, or any future service-contributed scope) — see {@code permission.scope} column.

methods.name: reload
methods.comment: Reloads the in-memory cache from ConfigCache.

methods.name: registerSite
methods.comment: Called by PlmPermissionValidator to record annotation usage sites.

name: com.plm.permission.PermissionRegistry$PermissionSite
comment: Annotation usage sites — populated by PlmPermissionValidator at startup.

name: com.plm.permission.ViewService
comment: Résolution des vues d'attributs en fonction du rôle et de l'état lifecycle.  Pipeline : 1. resolveActiveView — trouve la vue la plus prioritaire pour (rôle ∩ état) 2. applyViewOverride — applique les restrictions de la vue sur chaque attribut  Règle fondamentale : une vue peut RESTREINDRE mais JAMAIS élargir les droits définis par l'état lifecycle (AttributeStateRule).

methods.name: resolveActiveView
methods.comment: Résout la vue active pour un utilisateur dans un état donné.  Algorithme : 1. Chercher une vue éligible (role_id ∩ user_roles ET state_id = currentState) 2. Si plusieurs vues éligibles → prendre celle avec la priorité la plus haute 3. Si aucune vue → retourner null (comportement par défaut)

methods.name: isEligible
methods.comment: Replicates the SQL WHERE logic: (role matches AND state matches) OR (role matches AND state is null) OR (role is null AND state matches)

methods.name: applyViewOverride
methods.signature: applyViewOverride(java.lang.String, java.lang.String, boolean, boolean, int, java.lang.String)
methods.comment: Applique les overrides d'une vue sur un attribut.  Règle fondamentale : un override ne peut que RESTREINDRE. Si la règle d'état dit editable=false, la vue ne peut pas dire editable=true.

name: com.plm.permission.internal.NodeScopeKeyResolver
comment:
Resolves the {@code nodeType} key for {@code NODE}-scoped (and {@code LIFECYCLE}-scoped, via parent inheritance) permission checks. The annotation at the call site may supply {@code nodeType} directly, a {@code nodeId}, or a {@code linkId}; this resolver maps everything to {@code nodeType}.

Why a resolver and not SpEL? Because {@code nodeId → nodeType} requires a DB lookup in the scope of the current user's transaction, which can't be expressed in the annotation expression.

name: com.plm.permission.internal.PolicyService
comment: Legacy {@link PolicyPort} façade for the psm-api permission module. Methods delegate to the scope-agnostic {@link PolicyEnforcer} in platform-lib; translation to the psm-api {@link AccessDeniedException} lives here so call sites remain unchanged.

methods.name: resolveNodeTypeForTransition
methods.comment: Resolve the nodeType owning a given transition via its lifecycle. Any nodeType bound to that lifecycle qualifies — the caller only needs one.

name: com.plm.shared.action.ActionHandler
comment: PSM extension of the platform action handler. Adds {@code @AlgorithmType} for psm-admin algorithm catalog registration. All PSM action handlers implement this interface.

name: com.plm.shared.action.ActionScope
comment: PSM extension of the platform action scope. All PSM scope implementations implement this interface.

name: com.plm.shared.action.GlobalScope
comment: GLOBAL scope — no path IDs expected. For permission-only or system-level actions.

name: com.plm.shared.config.JacksonConfig
comment: Sérialise les Records JOOQ comme des objets JSON simples (via intoMap()). Nécessaire car Jackson ne sait pas introspécter les Records JOOQ directement.

name: com.plm.shared.event.OutboxPoller
comment: Polls event_outbox and delivers pending events via NATS, then deletes them.  Runs every 200 ms. Each poll is its own DB transaction: events are deleted only after the NATS publish succeeds, guaranteeing at-least-once delivery.  Uses FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED for multi-instance safety — two psm-api replicas polling the same DB won't double-deliver the same event.

name: com.plm.shared.event.PlmEventPublisher
comment: Publication d'événements PLM via le pattern outbox transactionnel.  Les événements sont écrits atomiquement dans event_outbox avec l'opération métier, puis lus par OutboxPoller et publiés sur NATS avant suppression.  NATS subjects : global.{eventType}                                    → tous les utilisateurs connectés project.{psId}.users.{userId}.{eventType}             → utilisateur ciblé env.service.{serviceCode}.{eventType}                 → interne inter-services

methods.name: enqueue
methods.comment: Inserts an event row into event_outbox within the current DB transaction. OutboxPoller picks it up after commit and publishes to NATS before deletion.

name: com.plm.shared.exception.AccessDeniedException
comment: Thrown when an authenticated user attempts an operation they are not permitted to perform. Results in an HTTP 403 response.

name: com.plm.shared.exception.GlobalExceptionHandler
comment:
Global exception handler — two distinct response shapes: FUNCTIONAL (expected business-rule violations, PlmFunctionalException subclasses or standard validation exceptions): 
 { "category": "FUNCTIONAL", "error": "Node X is locked by alice", "path": "POST /api/...", "status": 409 }
TECHNICAL (unexpected runtime errors): 
 { "category": "TECHNICAL", "error": "...", "type": "java.lang.NullPointerException", "path": "GET /api/...", "status": 500, "stackTrace": ["frame1", "frame2", ...] }

methods.name: handleNotFound
methods.comment: Quietly handle 404 on unmapped routes (e.g. federated fan-out probes from platform-api targeting axes this service doesn't expose).

name: com.plm.shared.exception.GuardViolationException
comment: Thrown when one or more guard preconditions fail at execution time. HTTP 422 — the request is syntactically valid but the action's preconditions are not met.

name: com.plm.shared.exception.PlmFunctionalException
comment: Base class for all expected, business-rule violations in the PLM domain.  Subclass this for any error that is part of normal operation (lock conflicts, guard failures, permission denied, validation errors…). The global exception handler returns these without a stack trace — they are presented to the user as plain error messages.  Contrast with unexpected technical exceptions (NullPointerException, DataAccessException, …) which bubble up as 500s with a full stack trace.

name: com.plm.shared.exception.UnauthenticatedException
comment: Thrown when a service method requires authentication but no security context is present on the current thread.  Handled by {@link com.plm.shared.exception.GlobalExceptionHandler} as HTTP 401.

name: com.plm.shared.hook.AtCommitHook
comment: Extension point called after validation passes but before the commit is persisted. Throwing from onCommit aborts the entire commit (transaction rolls back).  Register via: transactionService.registerAtCommitHook(hook)

methods.name: name
methods.comment: Unique name used as registration key.

methods.name: onCommit
methods.comment: Called in commit order. Throw to abort the commit.

name: com.plm.shared.hook.CommitContext
comment: Context passed to PreCommitValidator and AtCommitHook. Describes all node versions being committed in this transaction.

name: com.plm.shared.hook.CommitContext$NodeVersionRef
comment: Lightweight reference to a node version within the commit context.

name: com.plm.shared.hook.CommitResult
comment: Result passed to PostCommitHook after a successful commit. continuationTxId is non-null only if a partial commit created a new open transaction.

name: com.plm.shared.hook.CommitViolation
comment:
Structured validation error raised by a PreCommitValidator.

Identifier policy mirrors {@code ValidationService.Violation}: the attribute code ({@code attrCode}, slug) is the canonical identifier; the label ({@code attrLabel}) is for human display only.

name: com.plm.shared.hook.PostCommitHook
comment: Extension point called after a transaction has been successfully committed. Exceptions are caught and logged — they never cause the commit to fail.  Register via: transactionService.registerPostCommitHook(hook)

methods.name: name
methods.comment: Unique name used as registration key.

methods.name: afterCommit
methods.comment: Called after commit. Exceptions are swallowed (logged at WARN).

name: com.plm.shared.hook.PreCommitValidator
comment: Extension point called before a transaction commit is persisted. All violations across all validators are collected before throwing. A non-empty violation list aborts the commit.  Register via: transactionService.registerPreCommitValidator(validator)

methods.name: name
methods.comment: Unique name used as registration key.

methods.name: validate
methods.comment: Return violations; return empty list to pass.

name: com.plm.shared.metadata.Metadata
comment:
Declares that a class uses a specific metadata key on a given target type. At startup, {@link MetadataRegistry} discovers all beans annotated with {@code @Metadata} and builds a catalog of known metadata keys per target type. This catalog is exposed via API so the frontend can dynamically render metadata toggles. 
 {@literal @}Metadata(key = "frozen", target = "LIFECYCLE_STATE", description = "Blocks content modifications (checkout, attribute changes)") public class NotFrozenGuard implements Guard { ... }

methods.name: key
methods.comment: The metadata key (e.g. "frozen", "released").

methods.name: target
methods.comment: The target type this key applies to (e.g. "LIFECYCLE_STATE").

methods.name: description
methods.comment: Human-readable description shown in the admin UI.

name: com.plm.shared.metadata.MetadataRegistry
comment: Discovers all Spring beans annotated with {@link Metadata} at startup and builds a catalog of known metadata keys per target type.  This allows the frontend to dynamically render metadata toggles based on what the backend code actually supports.

methods.name: getKeysForTarget
methods.comment: Returns known metadata keys for a given target type.

methods.name: getAllKeys
methods.comment: Returns all known metadata keys.

name: com.plm.shared.metadata.MetadataService
comment: Generic key/value metadata attached to any entity via target_type + target_id.  Reads from ConfigCache (populated by psm-admin snapshots). Entity metadata is stored in ConfigSnapshot as a flat map with composite keys: "TARGET_TYPE:targetId:metaKey" → "metaValue"

methods.name: getMetadata
methods.comment: Returns all metadata for a given target, or empty map.

methods.name: getValue
methods.comment: Returns a single metadata value, or null.

methods.name: isTrue
methods.comment: Returns true if the metadata key exists and equals "true".

name: com.plm.shared.model.Enums$VersionPolicy
comment: Version policy on a NodeType — controls how the business version number (revision.iteration) advances when a content checkout is performed.  NONE    → same revision.iteration (traceability only, no visible increment) ITERATE → iteration + 1  (A.1 → A.2)  — default RELEASE → new revision, iteration reset to 1  (A.x → B.1)

name: com.plm.shared.model.ResolvedAttribute
comment: Fully resolved attribute definition, including inheritance metadata. Built from config snapshots provided by psm-admin, walking the parent_node_type_id chain.

name: com.plm.shared.model.ResolvedNodeType
comment: Fully resolved node type, including merged attributes from the inheritance chain. Built from config snapshots provided by psm-admin.

name: com.plm.shared.model.numbering.AlphaNumericNumberingStrategy
comment: ALPHA_NUMERIC scheme: single-letter revision (A, B, ... Z, AA, AB, ...) + numeric iteration.  Examples: ITERATE: A.1 → A.2 → A.3 REVISE:  A.3 → B.1 → ... → Z.1 → AA.1 NONE:    A.2 → A.2 (unchanged)  Stateless singleton — safe to share across threads.

name: com.plm.shared.model.numbering.NumberingResult
comment: Immutable result of a numbering computation — the business identity of a version.

name: com.plm.shared.model.numbering.NumberingStrategyFactory
comment: Static factory mapping a NumberingScheme identifier to its strategy implementation.  To add a new scheme: 1. Add its constant to Enums.NumberingScheme 2. Implement VersionNumberingStrategy 3. Add a case here

methods.name: forSchemeString
methods.comment: Convenience overload for raw strings coming from the DB. Null-safe; unknown values degrade gracefully to ALPHA_NUMERIC.

name: com.plm.shared.model.numbering.VersionNumberingStrategy
comment: Pure domain interface for pluggable version numbering schemes.  Implementations receive the VersionStrategy (NONE/ITERATE/REVISE) and the previous revision/iteration, and return the computed business identity for the new version.  No Spring annotations — plain POJO. Implementations should be stateless singletons.

methods.name: compute
methods.comment: Compute the new revision and iteration for a version being created.

name: com.plm.shared.security.PlmProjectSpaceContext
comment: Holder ThreadLocal du contexte de ségrégation (project space) courant. Initialisé par PlmAuthFilter à chaque requête HTTP depuis le header X-PLM-ProjectSpace.  Le project space filtre toutes les opérations sur les noeuds : un noeud n'est visible que si son project_space_id correspond au contexte actif.

methods.name: get
methods.comment: Retourne le project space ID courant, ou null si non défini.

methods.name: require
methods.comment: Retourne le project space ID courant. Lève une exception si le contexte n'est pas initialisé (requête sans header X-PLM-ProjectSpace).

name: com.plm.shared.security.PlmSecurityContext
comment: Holder ThreadLocal du contexte utilisateur courant. Initialisé par PlmAuthFilter à chaque requête HTTP. Accès externe via {@link ThreadLocalSecurityContext} (implémentation de SecurityContextPort).

name: com.plm.shared.security.PlmUserContext
comment: Contexte utilisateur courant pour une requête PLM.  Porté par un ThreadLocal via l'infrastructure (ThreadLocalSecurityContext). Contient l'identité de l'utilisateur et ses rôles résolus.  En production, ce contexte serait alimenté depuis un JWT / OAuth2 token. Pour l'instant il est initialisé manuellement ou via un header HTTP.

name: com.plm.shared.security.PnoProjectSpaceClient
comment: Lightweight client to resolve project space descendants from PNO. Uses the registry-aware ServiceClient for direct service-to-service calls. Cached 60s — hierarchy changes are rare.

methods.name: getDescendants
methods.comment: Returns the given space ID + all descendant space IDs. Cached 60s.

name: com.plm.shared.security.PsmAuthContextBinder
comment: Copies the shared {@link PlmPrincipal} into psm-api's local context holders ({@link PlmSecurityContext} + {@link PlmProjectSpaceContext}). The project space falls back to the {@code X-PLM-ProjectSpace} header when the JWT does not carry a {@code ps} claim.

name: com.plm.shared.security.PsmAuthzContextProvider
comment: Bridges {@link SecurityContextPort} + {@link PlmProjectSpaceContext} onto the platform-lib {@link AuthzContextProvider} SPI, so the shared enforcer can read user + project-space context without knowing about psm-api internals.

name: com.plm.shared.security.SecurityContextPort
comment: Port interface for accessing the current request's security context.  Domain services depend on this interface rather than directly accessing infrastructure ThreadLocals (PlmSecurityContext, PlmProjectSpaceContext).  Implemented by {@code ThreadLocalSecurityContext} in the infrastructure layer.

methods.name: currentUser
methods.comment: Returns the current user context. @throws IllegalStateException if no security context is set on the current thread

methods.name: currentUserOrNull
methods.comment: Returns the current user context, or {@code null} if not set. Used by the AOP aspect where absence of context means unauthenticated.

methods.name: currentProjectSpaceId
methods.comment: Returns the active project space ID, or {@code null} if not set.

methods.name: requireProjectSpaceId
methods.comment: Returns the active project space ID. @throws IllegalStateException if no project space context is set (missing X-PLM-ProjectSpace header)

name: com.plm.shared.security.ThreadLocalSecurityContext
comment: Implements {@link SecurityContextPort} by delegating to the ThreadLocal holders ({@link PlmSecurityContext} and {@link PlmProjectSpaceContext}) that are populated by {@link JwtAuthFilter} on each incoming HTTP request.

name: com.plm.shared.settings.SettingSection
comment: A pluggable settings section. Each module declares its own sections as Spring beans. The SettingsController collects all beans and exposes them filtered by user permissions.

methods.name: permission
methods.comment: Permission code required to see this section, or null if open to all.

name: com.plm.source.KeyHint
comment: One row of the key-picker autocomplete result.

name: com.plm.source.LinkConstraint
comment: Subset of link_type config relevant to resolver validation.  Built from {@code com.plm.platform.config.dto.LinkTypeConfig} on the call-site to avoid exposing the full DTO inside the resolver API.

name: com.plm.source.Reference
comment: One incoming reference returned by {@link SourceResolver#findReferencesTo}.  Used to build cross-source Where-Used views. SELF returns one row per parent node; other resolvers may return external references (e.g. an external system's change-set referencing a node).

name: com.plm.source.ResolvedTarget
comment: Outcome of {@link SourceResolver#resolve}.

name: com.plm.source.SourceQueryController
comment:
Read-only endpoints for the link picker UI:
  • {@code GET /sources} — list all sources from snapshot
  • {@code GET /sources/{code}/types} — types this source's resolver supports
  • {@code GET /sources/{code}/keys?type=&q=&limit=} — autocomplete keys for a given (source, type, query)
Source CRUD lives in psm-admin — these endpoints serve the runtime needs of the link create/edit form on psm-api.

name: com.plm.source.SourceResolver
comment: Resolves a link's {@code (type, key)} pair against the system declared by a Source.  Each Source in psm-admin is bound to one resolver instance. The SELF source uses a built-in resolver backed by NodeService/VersionService; custom sources (file system, git, external PLM, ...) plug in by implementing this interface and registering an {@code algorithm_instance} of type {@code algtype-source-resolver}.  Implementations are discovered as {@link com.plm.algorithm.AlgorithmBean} beans.

methods.name: code
methods.comment: Algorithm code, must match the {@code algorithm.code} row backing this resolver.

methods.name: supportedTypes
methods.comment: Object types this resolver knows how to handle. The SELF resolver returns all node-type IDs; a file resolver might return {@code List.of("filetype")}.

methods.name: resolve
methods.comment:
Look up the target referenced by {@code (ctx.type, ctx.key)}. For SELF nodes:
  • {@code key = "PART-A"} — V2M, returns the master node, {@code pinnedKey == null}
  • {@code key = "PART-A@3"} — V2V, returns version 3, {@code pinnedKey == "PART-A@3"}

methods.name: validate
methods.comment: Validate the target against link-type constraints. Used during link creation/update to reject invalid pairings (wrong node type, cycles, ...). Returns the violation list — empty list means valid.

methods.name: isVersioned
methods.comment: Whether the underlying Source carries a version axis on its objects. The SELF resolver returns {@code true} (PSM nodes have version_number); resolvers fronting immutable stores (dst data files, blobs, foreign URLs) return the default {@code false}. Used as a runtime sanity check against {@code source.is_versioned} and to gate which {@code linkPolicy} values are permitted on link types pointing at this source.

methods.name: suggestKeys
methods.comment: Suggest matching keys for a (type, query) pair — feeds the frontend key picker. Default implementation returns empty (free-text input only).

methods.name: findReferencesTo
methods.comment: Reverse lookup: which incoming links reference this {@code (type, key)} pair? Used by the cross-source Where-Used view. Default implementation returns empty — sources without a reverse index don't contribute parents.

name: com.plm.source.SourceResolverContext
comment: Input to {@link SourceResolver#resolve} and {@link SourceResolver#validate}.

name: com.plm.source.SourceResolverRegistry
comment: Looks up the {@link SourceResolver} bound to a Source by walking {@code Source → resolverInstance → algorithm → bean}.

methods.name: getResolverFor
methods.comment: Resolve by source id (e.g. "SELF").

methods.name: all
methods.comment: All resolvers registered as algorithm beans (regardless of whether a Source binds them).
